_meta.title: Value Compass — Important Funder Nodes (parent-rolled-up)
_meta.rollup_rule: Named investment vehicles are collapsed to their ultimate parent. Children listed under 'rolled_up_vehicles'. HongShan (ex-Sequoia China) treated as a SEPARATE parent since the 2023 legal split.
_meta.inclusion_rule: Included if (a) deep-pocket strategic (hyperscaler / sovereign / index manager / major strategic corporate), OR (b) backs 2+ of the 18 makers, OR (c) is a controlling/defining backer of a maker. Single-maker boutique VCs are intentionally excluded here and live as edge-only data. NOTE (v3): the rule is applied by BEHAVIOUR, not entity type - an individual angel qualifies if they meet (a)/(b)/(c). NFDG (Nat Friedman & Daniel Gross) is included because it backs 2+ makers. Other angels (Bezos, Iger, Lutkin, Field, Shaq, etc.) touch only one of the 18 and remain edge-only in each maker's lead_backers.

field_notes.makers_backed: Which of the 18 makers this parent has an equity/strategic stake in (rolled up across all its vehicles).
field_notes.maker_count: Count of the 18 backed. The concentration metric.
field_notes.flag: Why this node matters for a power-concentration / value-axis read.

_meta.pass_b_added: Each funder now carries key_people and a brief also_funds portfolio summary (what else they back + a stat). Funders remain UNSCORED context per your decision.
_meta.reputation_note: v1 reputation layer: 'notable_for' records factual, public associations of funders' key figures (sourced where available). Thin first pass - needs dedicated sourced research before any public launch. We state associations as fact; the Capital Lens lets users decide what concerns them.

funders.name: Nvidia
funders.flag: BROADEST CROSS-HOLDER. Circular money-flow: invests in customers who buy its chips (its Anthropic check was tied to $30B Azure spend + Nvidia hardware purchases). The single most important edge type to surface.
funders.also_funds: Almost entirely AI/compute-adjacent: robotics (Figure), cloud/compute (CoreWeave, Nebius), autonomous & biotech-AI (Recursion), quantum. Did 67 venture deals in 2025 and committed $40B+ to AI equity in 2026 - it backs the chips' own customers.

funders.name: Andreessen Horowitz (a16z)
funders.flag: Broadest pure-VC cross-holder; spans frontier + consumer/SMB layers.
key_people: Martin Casado / Anjney Midha / Sarah Wang (AI deals)
funders.also_funds: The broadest VC: AI/ML (~40%), B2B SaaS, fintech, 200+ bio/health, crypto/Web3 (Coinbase), American Dynamism/defense (Anduril), consumer (Airbnb). ~$46B AUM (#1 VC by AUM); raised $15B in Jan 2026 (~18% of all US VC dollars). Saudi PIF/Sanabil is an LP; politically tied to the Trump administration.
notable_for: Co-founders Marc Andreessen & Ben Horowitz
Publicly aligned with the Trump administration (Andreessen advised on tech policy; partner Scott Kupor became OPM director)
'American Dynamism' / techno-optimist thesis; major crypto backer

funders.name: Alphabet / Google
funders.flag: Funds its own competitor (Anthropic ~14%, capped 15%, no votes/board) while owning DeepMind. Sits on both sides of the open/closed and competitor map.
key_people: CapitalG, GV, Gradient, Google AI Futures Fund (vehicles)
funders.also_funds: Vast: owns DeepMind, Waymo (autonomous), Verily (health), Isomorphic (drug discovery); CapitalG backs late-stage SaaS. A $3T+ company funding its own competitor (Anthropic).

funders.name: Accel
funders.flag: Dominant on the consumer/SMB tool layer (led Perplexity $14B, Lovable Series A, Gamma seed/A, co-led Cursor).
funders.also_funds: Classic enterprise/consumer VC: Facebook, Slack, Dropbox, Atlassian, Spotify, Qualtrics. Now heavy on the AI application layer (Perplexity, Lovable, Cursor).

funders.name: Coatue
funders.flag: Crossover fund present at both frontier and tool layers; co-led Cursor's $29.3B round.
funders.also_funds: Crossover hedge fund spanning public + private tech; large AI book; historically Snap, Lyft, Rivian, ByteDance.

funders.name: Sequoia Capital (US)
funders.flag: Legally separate from HongShan (Sequoia China) since 2023 — kept as distinct nodes. Co-led Anthropic Series H, led ElevenLabs Feb 2026.
funders.also_funds: One of the most successful VCs ever: Apple, Google, Nvidia, Stripe, Airbnb, WhatsApp, Zoom. Now OpenAI, Anthropic, ElevenLabs.

funders.name: DST Global
funders.flag: Recurring late-stage megafund participant across frontier + tools.
funders.also_funds: Late-stage global growth: Facebook, Twitter, Spotify, Alibaba, ByteDance, Stripe. Increasingly AI.

funders.name: Fidelity
funders.flag: Mutual-fund crossover; its marks effectively reprice these private companies for retail funds.
funders.also_funds: Mutual-fund/asset-manager crossover holding late-stage private tech across the board; its marks reprice these companies for retail funds.

funders.name: Lightspeed Venture Partners
funders.flag: Raised a record $9B in Dec 2025; deepening frontier exposure.
funders.also_funds: Broad VC: Snap, Affirm, Rubrik, Faire, Mistral. Raised a record ~$9B in Dec 2025.

funders.name: MGX
funders.flag: HIGHEST SOVEREIGN CONCENTRATION — anchors all three top US frontier labs. Stargate founding partner; targeting >$100B AUM.
key_people: Sheikh Tahnoon bin Zayed Al Nahyan (Chair; UAE National Security Advisor, ~$1.5T empire)
funders.also_funds: Abu Dhabi AI vehicle (founding partners Mubadala ~$330B + G42), targeting >$100B AUM. Beyond the labs: Stargate ($7B), a $30B data-infra JV with Microsoft+BlackRock, $2B into Binance (crypto), TikTok. Parent Mubadala is a broad sovereign (early AMD investor; created GlobalFoundries).
notable_for: Chaired by Sheikh Tahnoon bin Zayed Al Nahyan, UAE National Security Advisor
State-security-linked sovereign capital; part of an estimated ~$1.5T Abu Dhabi empire

funders.name: Qatar Investment Authority (QIA)
funders.flag: Main Gulf backer of Anthropic; xAI Series E anchor; reached down into the tool layer (Replit Series D).
funders.also_funds: Qatar sovereign fund (~$500B+): broad global - Harrods, Volkswagen/Porsche stakes, Credit Suisse (historically), real estate. AI is a newer thrust.
notable_for: Qatari state sovereign fund; geopolitical/state-influence considerations

funders.name: T. Rowe Price
funders.flag: Public-market asset manager taking late-stage private stakes; led Canva's $40B round.
funders.also_funds: Public-market asset manager taking late-stage private stakes; led Canva's $40B round; broad crossover book.

funders.name: Databricks (Databricks Ventures)
funders.flag: Strategic-corporate investor concentrated on the application/tool layer.
funders.also_funds: Strategic-corporate investor concentrated on the data/AI application layer (Perplexity, Lovable, Replit); itself one of the most valuable private AI-data companies.

funders.name: Microsoft
funders.flag: DUAL NODE (maker + funder). Backs THREE of the labs - OpenAI, Anthropic, and Mistral - while shipping competing MAI models. Straddles both US frontier leaders and Europe's flagship.
funders.also_funds: Both maker and funder: ~$13B+ into OpenAI (~27%), a committed Anthropic backer, and a minor ~$16M strategic stake in Mistral (2024, Azure partnership). M12 backs broad enterprise. Widely held public company.

funders.name: SoftBank
funders.flag: OpenAI ~30% of SoftBank's portfolio after its $30B; S&P revised outlook to negative — single-bet liquidity-risk node.
funders.also_funds: Owns ~90% of Arm; Vision Fund backed Uber, DoorDash, Coupang and (infamously) WeWork. Now AI-concentrated - ~$64.6B into OpenAI, a Stargate partner; OpenAI is ~30% of its portfolio (S&P flagged liquidity risk).
notable_for: Masayoshi Son; high-conviction, highly leveraged megabets
Concentrated AI exposure (OpenAI ~30% of portfolio)

funders.name: Thrive Capital
funders.flag: Deepest single-company relationship in the dataset (OpenAI: $29B → $285B); OpenAI took a reciprocal stake in Thrive Holdings (Dec 2025).
funders.also_funds: Concentrated, high-conviction: Stripe, Instagram, GitHub, Spotify, Robinhood. OpenAI is its deepest bet; OpenAI took a reciprocal stake in Thrive Holdings.
notable_for: Founder Josh Kushner (brother of Jared Kushner); deepest single bet is OpenAI

funders.name: ICONIQ
funders.flag: Led Anthropic Series F; co-led ElevenLabs Series C; holds a secondary stake in Canva.
funders.also_funds: Multi-family office for tech billionaires (clients reportedly incl. Zuckerberg, Dorsey) + ICONIQ Growth fund; late-stage enterprise software.

funders.name: General Catalyst
funders.also_funds: Broad VC + 'transformation' strategy: Stripe, Snap, Anduril, Ramp; even acquired a US health system. Backs Mistral, Canva.

funders.name: Khosla Ventures
funders.flag: First institutional VC into OpenAI (2019).
funders.also_funds: Deep-tech/contrarian: first institutional VC into OpenAI; climate, fusion, bio, robotics, Commonwealth Fusion.
notable_for: Founder Vinod Khosla, outspoken on deep tech, climate and AI optimism

funders.name: Founders Fund
funders.also_funds: Contrarian deep-tech: SpaceX, Palantir, Stripe, Anduril; backs OpenAI and Anthropic.
notable_for: Co-founded by Peter Thiel
Thiel is a prominent conservative/Republican political donor
Defense/national-security tilt (Palantir, Anduril, SpaceX)

funders.name: Tiger Global
funders.flag: More cautious 2025-26 stance; publicly warned AI valuations are 'elevated… at times unsupported by fundamentals.'
funders.also_funds: Crossover that was hyper-active in 2021 (hundreds of deals); now more cautious, publicly warning AI valuations are elevated. Early OpenAI/Cohere exposure.

funders.name: Tencent
funders.flag: Anchors the Chinese-lab cluster; reported lead of DeepSeek's first external round.
funders.also_funds: Chinese tech giant (WeChat, games); vast global gaming/tech stakes (Epic, Riot, Supercell). Backs DeepSeek and Moonshot.
notable_for: Chinese tech giant; PRC data-governance and regulatory context

funders.name: Salesforce (Salesforce Ventures)
funders.also_funds: Strategic-corporate arm backing enterprise SaaS/AI that complements Salesforce (Cohere, Lovable, Anthropic, Hugging Face).

funders.name: Amazon
funders.flag: Now backs BOTH leading labs - like Microsoft - tying Amazon to Anthropic (its big bet) and OpenAI (the new $50B). The OpenAI deal is bundled with AWS compute, the same invest-in-your-own-customer pattern as Nvidia. Depth across the two frontier leaders.
funders.also_funds: Largest Anthropic investor (~$8B in, ~$74B paper) AND committed up to $50B to OpenAI in Feb 2026 ($15B initial + $35B contingent on an OpenAI IPO/listing), bundled with AWS Trainium compute. Alexa Fund makes smaller strategic bets.

funders.name: Saudi PIF / Humain
funders.flag: $3B into xAI Series E (now SpaceX shares). Building a full national AI stack; expect broader future holdings.
funders.also_funds: Saudi sovereign fund (~$900B+): Lucid, Uber, gaming (Savvy/Scopely), LIV Golf, Newcastle FC, NEOM. Humain is its purpose-built national AI stack ($3B into xAI).
notable_for: Saudi state fund (Gov. Yasir Al-Rumayyan); Kingdom's economic-diversification & influence vehicle
Subject to scrutiny over Saudi human-rights record

funders.name: GIC
funders.flag: Co-led Anthropic Series G.
funders.also_funds: Singapore sovereign fund; broadly diversified global portfolio (real estate, infrastructure, public + private equity).

funders.name: Temasek
funders.also_funds: Singapore state investor; tech-heavy global book (historically Alibaba, payments, biotech); backs OpenAI.

funders.name: ASML
funders.flag: DEFINING backer: largest Mistral shareholder (~11%, €1.3B); CFO on strategic committee. Single-maker but structurally controlling — and complicates Mistral's 'European sovereignty' story.
funders.also_funds: Not a fund - the Dutch EUV-lithography monopoly that every advanced chip depends on; took a strategic ~11% stake as Mistral's largest shareholder.
notable_for: Dutch EUV-lithography monopoly every advanced chip depends on; strategic (not financial) backer of Mistral

funders.name: High-Flyer
funders.flag: CONTROLLING parent of DeepSeek (founder Liang Wenfeng ~89.5%). DeepSeek's sole funder until the 2026 external round closes.
funders.also_funds: Chinese quantitative hedge fund; DeepSeek's parent and (until 2026) sole funder. Not a diversified venture backer.
notable_for: Founder Liang Wenfeng; Chinese quant hedge fund; DeepSeek's controlling parent

funders.name: Alibaba
funders.flag: Also an in-house maker via Qwen; strategic backer of Moonshot. Chinese-cluster concentration node.
funders.also_funds: Chinese tech giant; also an in-house AI maker via Qwen; strategic backer of Moonshot. Cloud, commerce, logistics.
notable_for: Chinese tech giant; also an in-house AI maker (Qwen); PRC governance context

funders.name: HongShan (ex-Sequoia China)
funders.flag: Distinct from Sequoia US since the 2023 split; anchors the Chinese consumer-AI layer.
funders.also_funds: China's top VC (legally split from Sequoia in 2023): ByteDance, Meituan, PDD, Pinduoduo. Anchors the Chinese consumer-AI layer (Moonshot).
notable_for: Neil Shen; China's leading VC; PRC market/governance context

funders.name: AMD
funders.flag: Chipmaker-as-investor (parallels the Nvidia pattern) on a smaller scale.
funders.also_funds: Chipmaker-as-investor (parallels Nvidia on a smaller scale); strategic stakes in AI firms that may run on its hardware (Cohere).

funders.name: Index managers (Vanguard / BlackRock / State Street)
funders.flag: DEEPEST, LEAST-VISIBLE LAYER. Largest economic owners of every public anchor, so they sit beneath most private cap tables indirectly. Passive, but real via governance votes. Model as a meta-node behind the hyperscalers, not as a direct maker edge.
funders.also_funds: They don't pick AI startups - they passively own ~5-10% each of every public anchor (Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, Nvidia, Meta), making them the deepest, least-visible owners of the whole ecosystem. BlackRock alone manages ~$11T+.
notable_for: Passive 'universal owners' of nearly every public anchor
Common-ownership / concentration concerns despite passivity

funders.name: NFDG (Nat Friedman & Daniel Gross)
key_people: Nat Friedman (ex-GitHub CEO; now co-leads Meta Superintelligence Labs)
Daniel Gross (ex-Apple AI, ex-SSI CEO; now at Meta)
funders.also_funds: Joint ~$1.1B fund (reportedly 4x'd in two years). Beyond the two makers it backs here it has held Coinbase, Figma, CoreWeave, Character.ai, and Ilya Sutskever's SSI. In mid-2025 Meta hired both partners to co-lead Meta Superintelligence Labs and bought a stake (up to ~49%) in NFDG's holdings; the fund stopped making new investments.
funders.flag: INDIVIDUAL-ANGEL node that meets the inclusion rule via test (b): backs 2 of the 18 (Perplexity, ElevenLabs). Note: Meta's 2025 buyout of NFDG holdings gives Meta indirect minority exposure to both - another Meta concentration tentacle.

modeling_notes: Edges should carry: stake type (equity / strategic-commitment / secondary / controlling), confirmed-vs-reported, and vintage.
Nvidia's 8-maker reach + the Amazon/Google 'book the markup as profit' loop are the two highest-signal money-flow stories.
Three sovereign funds (MGX, QIA, PIF) + two Singapore funds (GIC, Temasek) mean state capital touches every top frontier lab — a distinct 'concentration' color from VC.
Index-manager meta-node is the layer most people miss; render it behind the hyperscalers.
Defining/controlling single-maker nodes (High-Flyer→DeepSeek, ASML→Mistral, Amazon→Anthropic depth) should be visually weighted by stake size, not edge count.
